concerns.text: FR-007 requires a sensitivity analysis sweeping the depth cutoff over a 'range of low integer values' and outputting a JSON for 'each' cutoff. Task T020 hardcodes the sweep to specific values (2, 3, 4) without a mechanism to define a configurable range, failing to cover the full requirement scope.

The spec's Edge Cases explicitly require the test suite to detect if CPU throttling (cgroups) fails due to container permissions and fail the run with a specific error code. Tasks T016 (throttling) and T018 (ANOVA) do not include a specific task to implement this validity check or a corresponding test task to verify the abort behavior.

SC-001 requires reporting the 'ratio of slopes (graph vs. text)' as a confirmation metric. Task T018 (ANOVA implementation) only lists outputting F-statistic, p-value, and 95% CI, with no task assigned to calculate and report the specific slope ratio.

FR-005 mandates post-hoc Tukey tests to identify pairwise differences. While T019 mentions 'post-hoc Tukey tests', T035 (Polish) moves 'Levene/Shapiro variance validation' to a non-blocking phase. The plan's Complexity Tracking section mandates variance validation as a requirement for the ANOVA; deferring it risks the core analysis (T018) being implemented without mandatory assumption checks.
